chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C. P. No. D–1245 of 2017 [M/s. Labbaik (Pvt.) Ltd. Vs. Federation of Pakistan & another] DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Priority 1. For hearing of Misc. No.7542/2017 (Contempt). 2. For hearing of Misc. No.6508/2017 (Stay). 3. For hearing of main case..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 19.08.2024 Mr. Asim Mansoor Khan, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Kashif Hanif, Advocate for PEMRA. Mr. Khaleeq Ahmed, D.A.G..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. In pursuance of the order dated 12.08.2024, Mr. Asim Mansoor Khan, learned counsel for the petitioner, has sent a notice to the petitioner to seek fresh instructions, however, all in vain. He has filed the tracking report which shows that the notice was received, yet the petitioner through its representative has not bothered to contact the counsel. Today, this application under Rule 50 of Sindh Chief Court Rules has been filed wherein they have pleaded that the fresh instructions have not been given. This petition is against a show cause notice of 08.02.2017 and as such there are serious questions regarding the maintainability of this petition alone. The application is taken on record. Office is directed to assign CMA number to this application. Since the petitioner has lost interest in the proceedings as despite receipt of the notice the counsel was not contacted and also on the count that this petition is apparently not maintainable in view of the fact that this petition is against a show cause notice, the petition is dismissed alongwith listed applications. CHIEF JUSTICE JUDGE Asif
